data.title: Computer Age Management Services Surpasses 10 Million Policies in e-Insurance Accounts
data.description: Computer Age Management Services said it is managing over 10 million policies across 8 million e-Insurance Accounts, reflecting the growing shift by policyholders to digital platforms, according to a Thursday press release filed with the Indian bourses.

data.title: Singapore Central Bank Could Ease Monetary Policy in January — Market Talk
data.description: The continued cooling of Singapore's underlying inflation supports Nomura's view that the central bank could ease monetary policy settings later this month. The brokerage's FX strategy team. now assigns a 55% likelihood to the MAS easing its policy via a slight reduction in the slope of S$NEER appreciation, up from 40% previously. The MAS likely views current policy settings as restrictive given the risk posed by Trump's tariffs, say Euben Paracuelles and Charnon Boonnuch in a note. Nomura continues to expect 2025 average core inflation at 1.6% on year, near the lower end of MAS's forecast range of 1.5%-3.0%. However, it sees downside risks after a broad-based, sharper-than-expected decline in November from October. MAS is due to announce its policy decision no later than Jan. 31. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: BEST Gets Non-Compliance Notice From NYSE
data.description:
BEST said Friday it received a notice from the New York Stock Exchange that it is not compliant with the exchange's listing rules due to its failure to timely file its Form 6-K for the half-year ended June 30, 2024.

The company said its shares will no longer be listed upon completion of its privatization efforts.

data.title: Sainsbury's Delivers Upbeat Quarter; Backed Guidance Seen as Reassuring — Market Talk
data.description: Sainsbury's 3Q update was positive, with sales growth against a challenging comparison point, Shore Capital analysts Clive Black and Darren Shirley write. The British grocer's unchanged expectations for retail adjusted operating profit are seen as reassuring, they say. Moreover, adjusted operating profit for its financial services division is now guided to be around 30 million pounds, leading to a 10 million pound upgrade to the U.K. investment group's pretax profit estimate. The Argos business held its own, with a 1.4% drop in sales amid a tough market but growth in the Christmas period, the analysts say. Sainsbury's looks particularly attractive given significant assets and cash generation, together with market share gains and a dividend yield of 5.2%, they say. Shares are down 2.0% at 25.78 pounds. (michael.susin@wsj.com)

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Friday at 3 AM ET
data.description:
U.K. Pound, Bonds Slide; U.S. Stock Market Closed

Volatility in U.K. asset prices evokes memories of the 2022 market meltdown.

China's Central Bank Suspends Purchase of Treasury Bonds in Open Markets

The move comes as long-term yields hit fresh lows amid expectations of more monetary policy easing.

Investors are on edge as Friday's jobs report could make or break the stock-market rally

Rising Treasury yields are ratcheting up the anxiety for equity investors. Thursday's stock-market closure offered only a brief reprieve from the mounting tension.

The World's Bond Markets Are Uneasy. It's the U.S.'s Fault.

The yield on 10-year U.K. government debt rose to the highest since 2008, the latest casualty of a global rout spurred by rising yields on Treasury notes.

The Stock Market Is Looking for Its Next Boost. Earnings Won't Provide.

Companies across the board are less likely to surpass expectations, while stocks have already taken the past few years to reflect growing earnings and a growing economy.

L.A. wildfires have caused more than $135 billion in economic losses - and counting

Fires across Southern California are already one of the most damaging in the states history.

U.S. Mortgage Rates Climb to Highest Since July

U.S. mortgage rates are back at their highest level since last summer, frustrating home buyers waiting for a break from some of the steepest borrowing costs in more than a decade.

Why two Fed officials doubt the need for many more rate cuts

One official warned that "progress on inflation could continue to stall."

Bank of England Monitoring Gilt Market, But Sees Moves as Orderly, Says Breeden

"So far the moves have been orderly," Breeden said after a speech in Scotland. "So far so good."

U.N. Raises Global Growth Forecast, but Warns of Potential Inflation Risks

An increase in trade barriers would likely reignite price pressures, the organization says ahead of Trump's plan to impose new tariffs.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Friday at 3 AM ET
data.description:
TikTok Is Heading to the Supreme Court. How Meta and Snap Could Benefit from a Ban.

The Supreme Court will hear oral arguments from TikTok on Friday.

The Pacific Palisades Inferno Won't Engulf the Insurance Industry

The insurance industry has been thriving as climate change increases the demand for coverage.

Fox Must Face Smartmatic's Defamation Lawsuit, Appeals Court Rules

The voting-machine company seeks billions in damages.

BlackRock Withdraws From Climate Coalition, Backpedaling Again on ESG

The world's largest asset manager is latest Wall Street firm to quit climate groups.

Elon Musk's xAI Launches Stand-Alone Grok App

The AI startup launched its first stand-alone consumer app, as the company tries to catch up with more established players such as OpenAI and Google in the generative AI race.

Wells Fargo Taps JPMorgan Executive to Lead Its Credit-Card Business

Growth in credit cards has been a focus for CEO Charlie Scharf.

Delta Air Lines' Earnings Can Make or Break the Sector's Rally. 1 Metric Is Key.

Airline stocks have been flying recently, which makes earnings season tricky to navigate for investors.

Southwest Airlines CFO Tammy Romo to Retire as Airline Embarks on Turnaround

Romo is retiring after more than three decades with the carrier as it works on a turnaround after lagging behind some of its peers in recent years.

DHL Buys Into the Growing Retail Returns Business

The acquisition of a returns services provider comes as retailers are grappling with the higher volumes that shoppers are sending back.

KKR, PSP Investments Partner to Buy Stake in Two American Electric Power Companies

KKR is partnering with the Public Sector Pension Investment Board to buy a 19.9% interest in two units of American Electric Power for $2.82 billion.

data.title: Update: Market Chatter: Azul, Gol Parent Expected to Sign Memorandum of Understanding for Potential Deal
data.description:
(Updates with Gol's response in the final paragraph.)

Azul and Abra, the parent company of Gol, are expected to sign a memorandum of understanding for the potential merger of the two Brazilian airlines, news outlet Valor Economico reported Wednesday.

The objective of the memorandum is to discuss details that could pertain to the deal, the report said.

Azul and Gol declined to comment when contacted by MT Newswires.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: PTA-News: ADX Energy Ltd.: Variation of Loan Note Agreements
data.description:
ADX Energy Ltd.

by Vesna Blazevic

Business news for the stock market Wien (pta/10.01.2025/09:06) - "Extension to March 2026 provides financial flexibility to pursue multiple, immediate growth opportunities" Key Points: * The revised terms apply to twenty five (25) Loan Notes of A$50,000 each (A$1.25 million in total) issued in July 2023. Five (5) Loan Notes of A$50,000 each (A$250,000 in total) will be repaid on the original repayment date of 11 January 2025. * The repayment date of the remaining A$1.25 million principal is31 March 2026 with loan interest rate continuing to accrue. Existing options previously issued in relation to the Loan Notes will lapse on 11 January 2025. New options will be granted for Loan Note holders who have executed variation agreements. * The agreement by the Loan Note holders to extend repayment provides ADX with the financial flexibility to pursue multiple identified growth opportunities and reposition the Company for further drilling and development activities before requiring access to further capital. * ADX will immediately focus on the following near term value development and strategic growth opportunities: * further evaluation and testing of the Welchau-1 well, * further oil appraisal and development activities in Upper Austria, * pursuing a multi target - low risk, shallow gas exploration play in Upper Austria, * ongoing portfolio development in Upper Austria, * the potential licencing of its Sicily Channel exploration permit offshore Italy, and * an ongoing partnership formation program to fund ongoing activities. For more details please see the PDF file. data.title: SPX: S&P 500 Futures Fall Ahead of Jobs Data. Earnings Season Is Around the Corner
data.description:
Key points:
  • S&P 500 futures drop ahead of bell
  • Jobs data to spark broad volatility
  • Earnings season cranks up next week

December jobs are expected to come in at 164,000. Earnings season is next week and banks will kick it off.

  • S&P 500 futures were looking a bit lousy Friday morning as investors were getting ready to come back to work after Thursday was a day off in honor of the passing of the 39th US President Jimmy Carter. There isn’t much time to scramble around as today’s economic calendar brings a top-of-the-list report. The nonfarm payrolls, or simply jobs data, will show how many people found jobs in December.
  • Analysts have pinned hopes on the figure of 154,000 new hires, a marked downgrade from November’s 227,000. Still, some speculate that this figure might be on the low end due to the month’s holidays and potential extra effort by employers to recruit seasonal workers. In case of a surprise readout — a figure that’s too low or too high above the consensus view — stocks might get rattled due to elevated volatility.
  • Looking ahead to next week, it’s the start of the earnings season and — as is tradition — Wall Street’s top banks will be the ones to crank it up. Bellwethers of consumer and business sentiment JPMorgan , Wells Fargo , and Goldman Sachs all provide their December-quarter updates on January 15. UnitedHealth , the infamous health insurer, will be another highlight of the week.

data.title: Rheinmetall Did Not Provide Financial Details of the Share Acquisition
China has ample fiscal space to bolster growth this year
data.description:
China has ample fiscal policy space and tools to support economic growth this year and it will step up spending to spur investment, Vice Finance Minister Liao Min said on Friday.

More proactive fiscal policy can be expected in 2025, in terms of its strength, efficiency and timing, Liao said at a press conference.

"Facing new situations and problems in both internal and external environments, we have sufficient policy space and tools," Liao said.

The authorities will closely monitor the international and domestic situation and adjust policies to provide strong support for economic and social development.

In December, China's top leaders pledged to increase the budget deficit, issue more debt and loosen monetary policy to maintain a stable economic growth rate, according to a summary of the Central Economic Work Conference.

Reuters has reported that Beijing would target a budget deficit of 4% of gross domestic product this year, while maintaining an economic growth target of around 5%.

In 2024, local governments issued a total of 4 trillion yuan ($545.5 billion) in special bonds, including 3.9 trillion yuan under the annual quota and 100 billion yuan carried over from 2023, Liao said.

In addition, local governments issued 2 trillion yuan in special bonds to swap for hidden local debt by Dec. 18, he added.

($1 = 7.3326 Chinese yuan)

data.title: Serco Group Target Cut to 175p From 225p by Jefferies
Serco Group Cut to Hold From Buy by Jefferies
Bank Indonesia's Rupiah Priority May Limit Easing Cycle — Market Talk
data.description: Bank Indonesia's strong emphasis on foreign-exchange stability will likely make it difficult to deliver additional rate cuts in the coming months, Nomura analysts say. That's particularly true given that President-elect Trump is threatening to raise tariffs on imports and the Fed is turning less dovish, Euben Paracuelles and Nabila Amani say in a note. The economists pencil in one 25bp cut in 4Q 2025. The Indonesian central bank meets next week. They see Indonesia's GDP growth slowing slightly from an estimated 5.0% expansion in 2024. Household spending will likely be held back by still-weak consumer sentiment in 1H before edging up in 2H as public-sector spending improves. (fabiana.negrinochoa@wsj.com)

data.title: Kingswood Makes Interim CEO Permanent
data.description:
Kingswood said Friday it appointed its interim chief executive officer Peter Coleman as permanent.

Coleman has been interim CEO of the British wealth and investment management group since July 2024.

data.title: Updated Mineral Resources and Mineral Reserves for Kevitsa as well as grade guidance for 2025
data.description:
Boliden has updated the estimations for Mineral Resources and Mineral Reservesat Kevitsa, as a result of more detailed studies of the mineralization. Theupdated estimates show an increase in tonnage in both Mineral Resources andMineral Reserves compared to the previous year. The average grade for theMineral Reserve is estimated to increase to 0.22 percent for nickel (NiS*) andremains as previously 0.31 percent for copper. Nickel and copper guidancegradesfor 2025 are estimated at 0.17 percent and 0.23 percent respectively. Also,thetiming for a decision on a potential Stage 5 project has been postponed by acouple of years.

"We are happy to announce that we have been able to improve the overall mineplan for Kevitsa. As a result, we not only increase the Mineral Reserves andsecure a prolongation of the mine life, we have also been able to optimize theproduction in a way that pushes the decision point for a possible Stage 5project up to a couple of years forward. It is, however, important to stressthat European policymakers should act more decislivly in order to safeguardtheinternal nickel value chain from unfair and unsustainable competition," saysStefan Romedahl, President Boliden Mines.

Kevitsa

During 2024 a comprehensive re-estimation of the Mineral Resources has beenconducted which has resulted in a new mine plan and updated Mineral Reserves.Intotal 25 Mtonnes have been added to the Mineral Reserve. During 2024, a totalof9.85 Mtonnes were milled, making the net increase in Mineral Reserves 15Mtonnes(19%). The increase has resulted in an extended mine life to a total of 10years, given a pace of production in line with the environmental permit of 10Mtonnes per year.

In 2023, 20 Mtonnes were downgraded from Mineral Reserves. The same amount hasnow been converted back, related to the permit application to change theconstruction method for the Tailing Storage Facility (TSF). Thismethodologicalchange creates the possibility to increase the TSF to the capacity needed forthe current mine plan. The Mineral Resources have increased by 15 Mtonnes (9%)despite the above conversion back to Mineral Reserves. The increase isattributed to the re-estimation conducted in 2024, as well as theincorporationof new assumptions regarding costs, prices and terms.

The total volume as well as average grade for each classification is shown intable 1 below. For 2025, the average production grade will be 0.17 percent fornickel and 0.23 percent for copper, both under the average grade guidance forlife of mine plan. The reason for temporarily lower grades is a new andimprovedmine plan which requires mining in certain low-grade areas of the pit in orderto be able to reach better positions in the future.

Table 1. Mineral Resources and Mineral Reserves for Boliden Kevitsa Mine as of31-12-2024 and 31-12-2023 for comparison.(Tonnes and grades are rounded which may result in apparent summationdifferences).

2024 Ktonnes Au Cu NiS* CoS** Pt PdClassification (g/t) (%) (%) (%) (g/t) (g/t)Mineral ReservesProved 37,400 0.10 0.30 0.20 0.009 0.22 0.14Probable 60,100 0.09 0.32 0.23 0.010 0.17 0.11Total 97,500 0.09 0.31 0.22 0.010 0.19 0.12

Mineral ResourcesMeasured 69,100 0.08 0.29 0.20 0.010 0.15 0.10Indicated 111,300 0.07 0.34 0.23 0.010 0.12 0.07Total M&I 180,400 0.07 0.32 0.22 0.010 0.13 0.08Inferred 1,300 0.05 0.24 0.14 0.010 0.08 0.05

2023 Ktonnes Au Cu NiS* CoS** Pt PdClassification (g/t) (%) (%) (%) (g/t) (g/t)Mineral ReservesProved 47,900 0.10 0.31 0.20 0.009 0.20 0.13Probable 34,200 0.08 0.32 0.21 0.010 0.17 0.11Total 82,100 0.09 0.31 0.20 0.009 0.18 0.12

Mineral ResourcesMeasured 60,700 0.09 0.34 0.23 0.011 0.17 0.11Indicated 105,700 0.07 0.36 0.24 0.012 0.12 0.07Total M&I 166,400 0.08 0.35 0.23 0.011 0.14 0.09Inferred 300 0.04 0.22 0.13 0.011 0.06 0.03

(*Sulphidic Ni, **Sulphidic Co)

Additional information about Mineral Resource and Mineral Reserveclassificationis available at the Boliden web site (Mineral Resources and Mineral Reserves -Boliden (https://www.boliden.com/operations/exploration/mineral-reserves-and-mineral-resources/)) and an updated summary report about Kevitsa MineralResources and Mineral Reserves 2024 will be published there shortly. Thispressrelease about Kevitsa Mineral Resources and Mineral Reserves has been reviewedand approved by Sofia Höglund, Competent Person and Head of Mineral Resourcesand Project Evaluation, Boliden, who is a member of The FennoscandianAssociation for Metals and Minerals Professionals (FAMMP), an approvedorganization for competent persons according to PERC.

For further information, please contact:

Klas NilssonDirector Group Communications+46 70-453 65 88klas.nilsson@boliden.com

Metals for generations to come

Boliden's vision is to be the most climate-friendly and respected metalproviderin the world. We are Europe's producer of sustainable metals and, guided byourvalues care, courage and responsibility, we operate within exploration, mines,smelters and recycling. We are around 6,000 employees and have annual revenuesof approximately SEK 80 billion. The share is listed in the Large Cap segmentofNASDAQ OMX Stockholm.

data.title: Frasers Group Raises Stake in Marks Electrical Group
data.description:
By Anthony O. Goriainoff

Marks Electrical Group said Mike Ashley's Frasers Group increased its shareholding in the company to 7.8% from 6.4%.

The London-listed online electrical retailer said Friday that Frasers now owns 8.2 million shares, worth 48.2 million pounds ($59.3 million) based on Thursday's closing price of 586 pence.

Write to Anthony O. Goriainoff at anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com

data.title: Vodafone Sells Remaining 3% Share in Indus Towers; Increases Stake in India's Vodafone Idea
data.description:
By Najat Kantouar

Vodafone Group sold its remaining 3% stake in telecom tower company Indus Towers and used the 28.0 billion Indian Rupees ($325.9 million) raised in proceeds to repay debt and increase its stake in India's Vodafone Idea.

The U.K. telecommunication group said Friday that it has completed the placing of its 79.2 million shares in Indus Towers via an accelerated bookbuild offering, as previously announced in December.

Part of the proceeds, representing 8.9 billion Indian Rupees, have been used to fully repay its existing debt secured against Vodafone's Indian assets and settle transaction fees.

Additionally, 19.1 billion Indian Rupees have been used to acquire 1.7 billion equity shares in Vodafone idea, increasing Vodafone's stake to 24.39% from 22.56%.

Vodafone has been gradually reducing its stake in Indus Towers over time. In June, the group sold 484.7 million shares through an accelerated placing to pay off its existing debt of 1.8 billion euros ($1.89 billion) secured against Vodafone's Indian assets.

Write to Najat Kantouar at najat.kantouar@wsj.com

data.title: Market Chatter: Singapore Banks Backs Law to Boost Measures Against Scams
data.description:
Singapore Banks have given their backing to a new law that will allow greater control to Singaporean law enforcement agencies against those they suspect to be scam targets, Bloomberg reported Thursday, citing an emailed statement by Ong-Ang Ai Boon, director of Association of Banks in Singapore.

The Singapore government passed the protection from scams bill on Tuesday. The law comes after around SG$385.6 million was lost to scams and cybercrime activity in the city-state during the first half of 2024.

Under the new law, restriction orders will be issued to accounts that are vulnerable to scams.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Finland's Eevia Health Bags Bilberry Extract Order from US Customer
data.description:
Eevia Health (EEVIA.ST) said Friday that it received a 50,000-euro order for its bilberry extract from an existing US-based customer.

Before the order, the Finland-based plant extract manufacturer's Feno-Myrtillus 36 product completed a qualification procedure with the US nutraceutical company in the fall of 2024. The projected annual revenue from the product is 300,000 euros.

Eevia Health previously supplied the unnamed client with its Feno-Sambucus 14 elderberry extract from 2021 to 2023 but stopped in mid-2023 due to overstocking during the COVID-19 pandemic. The company expects to restart elderberry sales to the customer by the end of 2025.

data.title: Italy's Del Vecchio family raises Monte dei Paschi stake to 9.8%
data.description:
Key points:
  • Stake increase comes as Italian bank consolidation heats up
  • Del Vecchio family holds stakes also in Mediobanca, Generali
  • First invested in Monte dei Paschi in November

Late Italian billionaire Leonardo Del Vecchio's holding company has raised its stake in bailed-out lender Monte dei Paschi , a filing showed, strengthening its hand in expected consolidation among the country's banks.

The move by Delfin, the holding company of the late eyewear magnate and Ray-Ban owner, follows the Italian Treasury's placement of a 15% Monte dei Paschi (MPS) stake in November.

That sale handed Delfin a 3.5% stake. Francesco Gaetano Caltagirone, another Italian billionaire, also took 3.5%, while Banco BPM bought 5%.

The regulatory filing showed Delfin on Dec. 27, the day that MPS named five new directors, increased its holding to 9.78%.

The investment makes Delfin the biggest private investor in MPS after the Treasury, which holds 11.7% of the bank it rescued in 2017 - down from an initial 68%.

Italy's government had said the re-privatisation of MPS, which has restructured under CEO Luigi Lovaglio in recent years, should contribute to building a third large bank next to Intesa Sanpaolo and UniCredit .

The 15% placement raised the prospect of an eventual tie-up between MPS and Banco BPM and prompted UniCredit to launch a takeover bid for BPM, with CEO Andrea Orcel saying his bank could not afford to be sidelined as consolidation sped up.

UniCredit's bid for BPM threw a spanner in the works of BPM's own bid for fund manager Anima Holding .

Like Delfin, Caltagirone is positioned to play a role in any future bank M&A. The construction tycoon has raised his initial MPS stake to 4.97% and bought 5% of Anima. He now has two representatives on the MPS board, including son Alessandro Caltagirone.

Delfin and Caltagirone are also the leading investors in Milanese bank Mediobanca with stakes of 19.8% and 7.8%, respectively. They are also investors in insurer Generali with stakes of 9.9% and 6.9%.
